title: Expand My Account profile fields, plan options, expiration/grace handling, newsletter opt-in, and disclosure updates

requirements.text: Update the backend user account data model to persist the following fields per authenticated user: full name, date of birth (MM/DD/YYYY as text), city, state, email address, phone number (optional), newsletter opt-in (yes/no), selected plan option (including term/pricing variant), plan start timestamp (when payment is recorded as received), and plan expiration timestamp. Ensure the data is retrievable and updatable by the authenticated caller.

quotes: On the Account page that top box needs to ask the user for their Full Name, Date of birth MM/DD/YYYY format, City & State, Email address and Phone number (optional) . All that information should be stored right there in the users account page.
Should then ask " Would you like to join our Weekly Newsletter?" Yes or No and be recorded automatically if the answer is yes.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes get/update APIs that allow the authenticated caller to read and write all listed account fields
Phone number can be omitted without failing validation
Newsletter opt-in persists and is returned accurately on subsequent fetches

requirements.text: Add plan selection support for the following explicit plan options (including term variants) and persist the selected option in the backend: Free $0; Basic $9.99 a month; Basic $59.94 for 6 months (No auto renewals); Basic $109.89 for a year (No auto renewals); Premier $24.99 a month; Premier $149.94 for 6 months (no auto renewals); Premier $274.89 for a year (no auto renewals).

quotes: The drop down menu to choose plan level should include these options as well: Free $0, Basic $9.99 a month, $59.94 6 months, $109.89 a year, No auto renewals. Premier $24.99 a month, $149.94 for 6 months, $274.89 for a year, no auto renewals.

acceptanceCriteria: My Account plan dropdown shows all required options exactly (Free, Basic monthly/6-month/year, Premier monthly/6-month/year) with the listed prices and 'No auto renewals' text for 6-month and yearly options
Selecting a plan updates persisted backend account data for the current user
Reloading the app shows the previously selected plan option

requirements.text: Implement plan expiration lifecycle behavior: after payment is recorded as received for a non-Free plan, compute and store the plan expiration date; display it on My Account. On the expiration date, automatically downgrade the account to Free-plan access. Keep the account open for 1 additional week (grace period) with Free-plan access, then automatically delete the account and all associated user data from the system after the grace period ends.

quotes: Once the user chooses a plan a box should populate after payment is received that informs the user of when the account expires.
The user will have 1 week from the expiration date, with free plan level access to their account before they have to renew, or allow the account to close out.
On the date of expiration the account goes automatically to the free plan level access.
The account will stay open for 1 week before the account is auto deleted out of the system.

acceptanceCriteria: After payment is recorded for a paid plan, My Account shows a populated expiration date
If the current time is past expiration, the effective plan is treated as Free (feature gating uses Free access) even if a paid plan was previously selected

If the current time is past expiration + 7 days, the backend deletes the user’s stored account/profile data and associated user-owned data (e.g., journal entries, mood entries) and subsequent fetches behave like a new/empty account

requirements.text: Update the My Account page UI so the top "Account Information" box becomes an editable form that collects and displays: Full Name, Date of Birth (MM/DD/YYYY), City, State, Email address, Phone number (optional), Plan enrolled in, and Plan expiration date. Provide Save behavior that persists changes in the backend and rehydrates correctly on refresh.

quotes: Under these links there is a fillabe box that should house the users name, Date of birth MM/DD/YYYY format, City & State, Email address, Plan enrolled in, Plan expiration date.

acceptanceCriteria: User can edit the required fields and save them
Date of Birth input enforces or validates MM/DD/YYYY format with user-facing error messaging on invalid format
Saved values display in the same My Account form after reload

requirements.text: Update authenticated navigation so the user has three clear clickable top-level destinations available in-app: Dashboard, My Account, Tools; ensure they are visible/accessible from within the My Account experience as described.

quotes: So the account page should have 3 clickable buttons on the top dashboard, My account, Tools.

acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated header/nav shows Dashboard, My Account, and Tools navigation items
Clicking each item navigates to the correct page without a no-op

requirements.text: Add a My Account UI control for the newsletter question: "Would you like to join our Weekly Newsletter?" with Yes/No selection, and persist the user’s selection to the backend (recorded automatically when the answer is Yes).

quotes: Should then ask " Would you like to join our Weekly Newsletter?" Yes or No and be recorded automatically if the answer is yes.

acceptanceCriteria: The exact question text is displayed on My Account
Selecting Yes persists newsletterOptIn=true in backend; selecting No persists newsletterOptIn=false (or unset) in backend
The persisted selection is reflected when revisiting My Account

requirements.text: Update the in-app disclosure/legal content to add the new rules described by the user, including plan non-auto-renew language for the new plan options and the expiration/downgrade/grace-period/auto-deletion policy.

quotes: Add new rules to the disclosure information.

acceptanceCriteria: The Legal & Policies content on My Account includes text covering: non-auto-renew for 6-month and yearly options, plan expiration behavior, downgrade to Free on expiration date, 1-week grace period, and auto-deletion after the grace period

requirements.text: Implement any required Motoko state migration so existing stored user profiles (currently only containing name) are safely upgraded to the new persisted account/profile structure with sensible defaults for newly added fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Upgrading the canister does not trap due to old profile data shape
Existing users keep their previously saved name value after upgrade

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; do not introduce additional backend services.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose existing shadcn components instead).
Do not modify any frontend paths marked immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Do not integrate real payment processing (Stripe or other third-party payment providers).
Do not implement email sending or any email-dependent flows.
Do not redesign unrelated pages (e.g., Landing page) beyond what is required to add the requested My Account and disclosure changes.
